description: Telemetry collection, aggregation, and privacy rules. 8 rules governing what data is collected, how it is aggregated, and privacy constraints.

rules.description: NEVER collect or transmit secret values, credentials, or API keys in telemetry data
rules.rationale: Telemetry may be stored in centralized systems with different access controls

rules.description: ALWAYS aggregate telemetry by session and skill name, never by individual tool call arguments
rules.rationale: Aggregation prevents reconstruction of sensitive operations from telemetry

rules.description: ALWAYS hash or anonymize user identifiers in telemetry before aggregation
rules.rationale: Privacy protection: telemetry should not be personally identifiable

rules.description: NEVER include file paths, source code snippets, or document content in telemetry
rules.rationale: File paths and code may reveal proprietary or sensitive information

rules.description: ALWAYS apply differential privacy (epsilon <= 1.0) when reporting aggregate statistics
rules.rationale: Differential privacy prevents identification of individual contributions

rules.description: ALWAYS respect telemetry opt-out preferences — if user opts out, collect only error telemetry
rules.rationale: User consent is required for non-essential data collection

rules.description: NEVER retain raw telemetry logs beyond 30 days; aggregate and purge
rules.rationale: Data minimization principle: don't keep data longer than necessary

rules.description: ALWAYS encrypt telemetry data in transit and at rest
rules.rationale: Encryption protects telemetry from interception and unauthorized access
